name: Compose Post
description: Post-composition surface. Modal-style top action bar with Cancel / Post commits (NOT a `navigation-bar` sub), two destination pickers (channel + identity), title input, an inline coaching banner, and two checkbox-style toggle rows above the keyboard. Keyboard-first layout: the upper half does all the structural work.

actionBar.note: Top action bar (modal, NOT `navigation-bar/*`). Leading `button/text` 'Cancel' (neutral) and trailing `button/text` 'Post' (accent blue when valid). Modeled here by the trailing 'Post' button; the matching 'Cancel' lives on the leading side.

channelPicker.note: Channel picker row — leading 4-dot grid icon, label, trailing chevron-down. Tapping this row opens [[compose-channel]] as a bottom sheet. This row is the paired trigger for that overlay.

identity.note: Identity row (read-only here, no chevron). Format is 'company · alias'.

title.note: Full-width title input, no chrome. Renders at `sys.typo.heading.md` bold.

props.body: Get people thinking! Use the title section for open-ended questions, and avoid loaded ones or those with definite answers.

guidance.note: Inline coaching banner sitting in the form stack, soft surface, secondary text color. Not modal.

options.note: Option-toggle row on the sunken surface band. Paired with the right-aligned `hideCompanyName` region — the two rows intentionally differ in alignment ('Invisible to Coworkers' leads with a soft band; 'Hide company name' is right-aligned for de-emphasis). Do not normalize.

hideCompanyName.note: Right-aligned `button/check` medium. Same component as the `options` region — only the host row and alignment differ.

rules: Top action bar is NOT a `navigation-bar` sub — do not retrofit `navigation-bar/page` onto it. It is a modal action bar with two `button/text` commits (Cancel + Post).
Channel picker row must open [[compose-channel]] as a bottom sheet — that overlay's trigger lives in this recipe.
Do not normalize the alignment difference between the two `button/check` rows — it's intentional emphasis.
